name: keychainbreaker

documentation.readme: Go library for reading and decrypting macOS Keychain files (login.keychain-db). Supports OS X 10.6 (Snow Leopard) through macOS 26 (Tahoe).
usage_sections: Generic passwords -- app-stored credentials (Chrome Safe Storage, Wi-Fi, etc.)
Internet passwords -- web and network credentials (GitHub tokens, Docker registry, SMB shares, etc.)
Private keys -- RSA/EC private keys stored in the keychain

arguments.name: --count
arguments.description: Number of packets, frames, or attempts to send.

arguments.name: --file
arguments.description: CLI flag '--file' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --format
arguments.description: CLI flag '--format' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --key
arguments.description: CLI flag '--key' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --password
arguments.description: Password for authentication (prefer --password-file).

arguments.name: --size
arguments.description: CLI flag '--size' accepted by the tool; behavior is implementation-defined and should be confirmed against the upstream README.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: [![Go CI](https://github.com/moonD4rk/keychainbreaker/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/moonD4rk/keychainbreaker/actions/workflows/ci.yml) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/moonD4rk/keychainbreaker/.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Go library for reading and decrypting macOS Keychain files (login.keychain-db). Supports OS X 10.6 (Snow Leopard) through macOS 26 (Tahoe).
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: brew install moond4rk/tap/keychainbreaker; go install github.com/moond4rk/keychainbreaker/cmd/keychainbreaker@latest. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `brew tap moond4rk/tap`. Further invocations from upstream: `go install github.com/moond4rk/keychainbreaker/cmd/keychainbreaker@latest`; `keychainbreaker -h`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use keychainbreaker because [![Go CI](https://github.com/moonD4rk/keychainbreaker/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/moonD4rk/keychainbreaker/actions/workflows/ci.yml).
When you need a concrete invocation, run `brew tap moond4rk/tap` as documented upstream for keychainbreaker.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `go install github.com/moond4rk/keychainbreaker/cmd/keychainbreaker@latest` as documented upstream for keychainbreaker.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `keychainbreaker -h` as documented upstream for keychainbreaker.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Keychain: /Users/user/Library/Keychains/login.keychain-db` as documented upstream for keychainbreaker.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair keychainbreaker with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `keychainbreaker --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
